questions.question: Define an angle.
questions.answer: An angle is formed when two rays meet at a common point called the vertex.

questions.question: What is an acute angle?
questions.answer: An angle whose measure is greater than 0° but less than 90°.

questions.question: What is a right angle?
questions.answer: A right angle is an angle measuring exactly 90°.

questions.question: What is an obtuse angle?
questions.answer: An obtuse angle is an angle greater than 90° but less than 180°.

questions.question: Define complementary angles.
questions.answer: Two angles whose sum is 90° are called complementary angles.

questions.question: Define supplementary angles.
questions.answer: Two angles whose sum is 180° are called supplementary angles.

questions.question: What are adjacent angles?
questions.answer: Adjacent angles are two angles that share a common vertex and a common arm.

questions.question: What is a linear pair of angles?
questions.answer: A linear pair consists of two adjacent angles whose non-common arms form a straight line.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles in a linear pair?
questions.answer: The sum of angles in a linear pair is 180°.

questions.question: What are vertically opposite angles?
questions.answer: Angles opposite each other when two lines intersect are called vertically opposite angles.

questions.question: State the theorem of vertically opposite angles.
questions.answer: When two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: What is a straight angle?
questions.answer: A straight angle measures 180°.

questions.question: What is the sum of angles around a point?
questions.answer: The sum of all angles around a point is 360°.

questions.question: What are parallel lines?
questions.answer: Parallel lines are lines in the same plane that never intersect.

questions.question: What is a transversal?
questions.answer: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more lines at different points.

questions.question: What are corresponding angles?
questions.answer: Corresponding angles are angles in the same relative position when a transversal cuts two lines.

questions.question: What are alternate interior angles?
questions.answer: Alternate interior angles are angles on opposite sides of a transversal and between the two lines.

questions.question: If corresponding angles are equal, what can be concluded?
questions.answer: The lines cut by the transversal are parallel.

questions.question: If alternate interior angles are equal, what can be concluded?
questions.answer: The two lines are parallel.

questions.question: What type of angle is 45°?
questions.answer: 45° is an acute angle.

questions.question: What type of angle is 120°?
questions.answer: 120° is an obtuse angle.

questions.question: What type of angle is 180°?
questions.answer: 180° is a straight angle.

questions.question: What type of angle is 200°?
questions.answer: 200° is a reflex angle.

questions.question: Give one example of complementary angles.
questions.answer: 30° and 60° are complementary angles.

questions.question: Give one example of supplementary angles.
questions.answer: 100° and 80° are supplementary angles.
